items.title: BoozerAnalytic JAX route
items.oracle_contract: byte-identity parity vs simsopt.field.boozermagneticfield.BoozerAnalytic for modB, G, I, iota, psip, K and all first derivatives at rtol=1e-12, atol=1e-14 on QA/QH/finite-beta fixtures
downstream_consumers: simsopt.field.tracing.trace_particles_boozer (duck-types accepted; downstream kernel wiring is N03 §D pending)
items.validation_command: JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu SIMSOPT_BACKEND_MODE=jax_cpu_parity SIMSOPT_BACKEND_STRICT=1 SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/field/test_boozer_analytic_jax.py
items.residual_risk: Near-axis singularity (1/sqrt(s) in dmodBds) matches CPU semantics; tests avoid s=0. Downstream tracing wiring is pending (recorded in N03 §D). The transfer-guard-clean kernel rewrite uses `state.scalar + (s - s)` and `s - s` patterns in _eval_iota / _eval_diotads / _eval_dGds / _eval_dIds; these propagate NaN from `s` whereas the CPU oracle writes a constant regardless of `s`. The divergence is unobservable on finite inputs (s - s = 0 in IEEE 754); document if downstream consumers ever pass NaN-bearing point grids.

items.title: InterpolatedBoozerField JAX wrapper
items.oracle_contract: scalar parity vs simsopt.field.boozermagneticfield.BoozerAnalytic for 14 implemented scalars (modB, dmodBdtheta/zeta/s, K, dKdtheta/zeta, G, I, iota, dGds, dIds, diotads, psip) at rtol=1e-5 atol=1e-7 (degree-6 Lagrange truncation budget on 8x8x8 grid). Routing tests cover the remaining 19 scalars via KeyError contract on un-built specs.
items.resolution_path_taken: Re-fit on host-resident grid via simsopt.jax_core.regular_grid_interp.build_regular_grid_interpolant_3d (originally documented resolution_paths[1]). NO C++/pybind11 changes. NO simsoptpp rebuild. The wrapper mirrors the C++ InterpolatedBoozerField lazy-build semantic via per-scalar callbacks into the base field's getter methods.
upstream_source_refs: src/simsoptpp/boozermagneticfield_interpolated.h:1-1046 (C++ class; exploit_fluxfunction_points, exploit_symmetries_points, apply_odd_symmetry, apply_even_symmetry, fbatch_scalar)
downstream_consumers: simsopt.field.tracing.trace_particles_boozer (the wrapper exposes frozen_state + psi0 so the existing duck-typed acceptance accepts it; full RHS routing requires the specific scalars to be pre-built)
items.validation_result: 39 passed (35 base + 4 Crucible iteration-2 additions closing the 3-vector symmetry test gap)
items.residual_risk: Host-resident spec arrays (cell_table, cell_to_row, mesh) get re-lifted to JAX on every evaluate_batch call. This is acceptable for construction-time semantics but means the wrapper does NOT operate cleanly inside `jax.transfer_guard('disallow')` blocks during evaluation. Documented in the test docstring. Pre-lifting to JAX is a follow-up optimization that does not affect numerical correctness or compose-strict-mode acceptance.

items.title: Boozer field tracing route inventory

subclass_inventory.BoozerAnalytic: CPU rejected; JAX wrapper BoozerAnalyticJAX (N01) accepted by tracing — full RHS dispatch via N03 §D _boozer_field_evaluators
subclass_inventory.BoozerRadialInterpolant: CPU rejected; JAX wrapper from item 33 (full coverage)
subclass_inventory.InterpolatedBoozerField: CPU rejected; JAX wrapper InterpolatedBoozerFieldJAX (N02) — host-resident refit path, no C++ rebuild

items.non_boozer_dispatch_safety: Cartesian families (BiotSavart, ToroidalField, PoloidalField, ScalarPotentialRZ, MagneticFieldSum/Multiply, NormalField) never enter Boozer-only conversion code.
items.fail_fast_contract: _resolve_boozer_field_state in src/simsopt/jax_core/tracing.py raises TypeError on any input lacking frozen_state and psi0 attributes. N03 §D _boozer_field_evaluators also raises TypeError on unknown frozen-state types.

section_D_wiring.summary: _BOOZER_RHS_EVAL_KEYS SSOT tuple + _boozer_field_evaluators(state) static dispatch in jax_core/tracing.py. The 3 RHS factories (vacuum, no-K, full) call the dispatch at factory-construction time (outside the JIT trace) and capture the bound evaluator dict by closure. Lazy imports inside the helper break the simsopt.field.boozermagneticfield_jax <-> simsopt.jax_core.tracing import cycle.
section_D_wiring.oracle_contract: Type-1 oracle (CPU BoozerAnalytic) for the 12 scalar values composed with type-2 oracle (closed-form vacuum/no-K/full guiding-centre RHS algebra from simsoptpp/tracing.cpp). JAX dispatch matches at rtol=1e-12, atol=1e-14.

items.validation_result: Item-33 regression + N03 §D dispatch tests both pass.
items.residual_risk: None for this scope. New frozen-state types (e.g. for InterpolatedBoozerFieldJAX with the full 34-scalar surface) would need a new dispatch arm in _boozer_field_evaluators.

items.title: SurfaceGarabedian / SurfaceHenneberg JAX specs

N04a_surface_garabedian.oracle_contract: byte-identity parity (rtol=0, atol=0) vs SurfaceGarabedian.to_RZFourier().rc/zs across 4 (mmin, mmax, nmin, nmax) shape fixtures

owner_files: src/simsopt/jax_core/surface_henneberg.py (gamma + gammadash1 + gammadash2 + normal + unitnormal + area + volume from spec)
N04b_surface_henneberg.oracle_contract: Direct-kernel parity vs SurfaceHenneberg.gamma()/gammadash1()/gammadash2()/normal()/unitnormal()/area()/volume() at rtol=1e-12, atol=1e-14 (gamma + first derivatives) and rtol=1e-11, atol=1e-13 (derived geometry). Coverage spans 12 (mmax, nmax, alpha_fac) combinations, 4 nfp values (1, 2, 3, 5), default and custom quadpoint grids, axisymmetric closed-form (R=1+0.1cosθ, Z=0.1sinθ), JIT-cache discrimination on alpha_fac meta_field, and strict transfer-guard execution.
upstream_source_refs: src/simsopt/geo/surfacehenneberg.py:21-93 (CPU class + docstring with Henneberg-Helander-Drevlak formulas)
N04b_surface_henneberg.validation_command: JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu SIMSOPT_BACKEND_MODE=jax_cpu_parity SIMSOPT_BACKEND_STRICT=1 SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/geo/test_surface_henneberg_jax.py

items.validation_command: JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu SIMSOPT_BACKEND_MODE=jax_cpu_parity SIMSOPT_BACKEND_STRICT=1 SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/geo/test_surface_garabedian_jax.py tests/geo/test_surface_henneberg_jax.py
items.residual_risk: Both Garabedian (N04a) and Henneberg (N04b) surfaces now have first-class pure-JAX spec-driven evaluators. The N04b kernels cover the four CPU public methods (gamma, gammadash1, gammadash2 + derived normal/unitnormal/area/volume). Second derivatives (gammadash1dash1, gammadash1dash2, gammadash2dash2) are intentionally out of scope — the CPU SurfaceHenneberg class itself does not implement them. Downstream consumers requiring second-derivative geometry should either upstream a CPU implementation first or route through SurfaceXYZTensorFourier.

items.title: CurveXYZFourierSymmetries immutable JAX spec
items.oracle_contract: Spec-driven gamma byte-identical to curve.gamma() at direct-kernel rtol=1e-12 atol=1e-14; derivative-heavy lane for gammadash/gammadashdash; multiple (nfp, ntor) coprime pairs covered.
downstream_consumers: Any consumer of curve_geometry_from_dofs / curve_pullback_from_dofs on this curve type
items.residual_risk: The strict-env-var SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow invocation fails at JaxCurve construction time (curve.py:991 jnp.ones_like(numpy)); this is a pre-existing JaxCurve constraint shared by every JaxCurve subclass (including JaxCurveXYZFourier) and is not specific to N05. The standard test pattern uses `with jax.transfer_guard('disallow'):` inside the test body, which N05's test_spec_geometry_runs_under_strict_transfer_guard already does.

items.title: SurfaceXYZTensorFourier clamped_dims support
items.oracle_contract: Gamma + first derivatives byte-identical to C++ SurfaceXYZTensorFourier for all 8 clamped_dims combinations under stellsym and non-stellsym
upstream_source_refs: src/simsoptpp/surfacexyztensorfourier.cpp (clamped basis function E(phi,theta) = sin(nfp*phi/2)^2 + sin(theta/2)^2)
src/simsopt/geo/surfacexyztensorfourier.py:188-205 (was the NotImplementedError; now threads clamped_dims through)
downstream_consumers: BoozerSurfaceJAX (M4) and downstream pure-JAX surface objectives
items.validation_command: JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu SIMSOPT_BACKEND_MODE=jax_cpu_parity SIMSOPT_BACKEND_STRICT=1 SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/geo/test_surface_xyz_tensor_clamped_jax.py
items.residual_risk: None for this scope; full 8-combo parity coverage of clamped_dims under both symmetries.

items.title: LinkingNumber JAX-native kernel
owner_files: src/simsopt/geo/curveobjectives.py (LinkingNumber.J gated on is_jax_backend with target-lane bypass guard)
items.oracle_contract: Byte-identity (integer-valued) vs sopp.compute_linking_number on linked/unlinked/near-coplanar pairs and multi-curve aggregations
items.validation_command: JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu SIMSOPT_BACKEND_MODE=jax_cpu_parity SIMSOPT_BACKEND_STRICT=1 SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/geo/test_linking_number_jax.py
items.residual_risk: jnp.round (banker's) vs std::round (half-away-from-zero) can differ on adversarial near-half-integer values; physical homotopy invariants stay far from half-integers so parity is stable on tested cases.

items.title: MHD fixed-output post-processing inventory
items.residual_risk: VMEC/SPEC/BOOZ_XFORM/virtual_casing wrappers remain external_solver_wrapper; pure-array postproc kernels surfaced as portable_gap candidates pending explicit promotion.

items.title: MagneticField composition strict-JAX guard
owner_files: src/simsopt/field/circular_coil_jax.py + 9 other JAX field classes (_simsopt_jax_native_field = True markers)
items.oracle_contract: MagneticFieldSum / MagneticFieldMultiply construction raises in strict JAX mode if any child lacks the _simsopt_jax_native_field marker; native-field-only composition passes
downstream_consumers: Stage-2 / single-stage outer loops that compose JAX-backed B / dB_by_dX evaluators
items.validation_result: 23 passed (strict env runs the TestStrictJAXModeFailFast class; without strict env vars 5 of those tests skipif-deselect by design)
items.residual_risk: Skipped tests cover field families that are still CPU-only (e.g., InterpolatedBoozerField). They re-activate automatically when N02 or future ports add the missing wrappers.
items.plan: .artifacts/native_remaining_jax_port_goal/plans/N09.md (may be terse; agent rate-limit cut summary short)

items.title: Generic solver orchestration inventory (serial.py / mpi.py)
items.decision: Generic SciPy/MPI host wrappers accept arbitrary Optimizable graphs whose residuals may be backed by simsoptpp/VMEC/SPEC/virtual_casing; they mutate prob.x and emit log files; MPI wrappers require mpi4py (excluded from JAX porting). No portable contract for a generic JAX opt-in wrapper. Existing strict opt-in wrappers (permanent_magnet_optimization_jax, wireframe_optimization_jax) are the precedent for narrow-objective JAX routes.

items.title: Live PermanentMagnetGrid host-loop workflow closure
items.decision: Deferred decision marker ratified. The live mutable PermanentMagnetGrid host loop is host orchestration; the supported native contract is the immutable PermanentMagnetGridJAX payload (item 28). No new CPU-style dispatcher is exported; the existing fail-fast non-claim test enforces this.

items.title: QfmSurface host solver orchestration inventory
items.decision: QfmSurface is host orchestration (SciPy minimize over surface.x mutation at qfmsurface.py:49/73/97/133/170). QfmResidualJAX already exists at surfaceobjectives_jax.py:691 with full CPU/JAX parity coverage. No missing residual kernel.

items.title: MGrid I/O inventory
items.decision: MGrid is a NetCDF read/write library + matplotlib plot helper. No file parsing/writing path is a JAX-port target. Zero JAX consumers today.

items.title: fourier_interpolation utility inventory
items.decision: Pure NumPy host utility with zero non-test consumers in src/simsopt/ (including jax_core). Only importer is its own unit test. Any prior 'Boozer blocker' claim is rejected on current evidence.
